summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has a harness boundary audit that detects target-project file mutations by full-tool Claude agents and blocks PRD readiness on violations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA-256 digests of all project files excluding allowed roots (artifact directories); diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: These functions are the core boundary audit mechanism that can detect any file mutation outside allowed write roots.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshot before agent execution, compares after execution, converts violations into BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, and prevents PRD readiness when violations exist.
evidence.why: This is the runtime integration that makes boundary violations gate PRD readiness instead of being silent.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers are mapped from diff changes, added to readiness blockers, and force prd_ready=false when any violations exist.
evidence.why: Explicit blocker injection ensures boundary violations block PRD readiness regardless of agent results.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' proves that agent execution with writeFileSync mutation results in status='blocked', boundary.project_mutation='violated', and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: This test confirms the boundary audit actually detects and blocks violations even when agents return passing results.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' shows boundary works with git repos, detecting untracked files added during agent execution.
evidence.why: Git-backed boundary detection provides stronger coverage for repos under version control.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' proves boundary audit works without git, detects modifications, and correctly ignores writes to allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Non-git fallback ensures boundary protection works even in non-versioned projects.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan declares execution_policy.boundary.project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots as the evidence artifact directory only.
evidence.why: The dispatch plan explicitly declares the boundary contract before any agent execution.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt hardcodes agent instructions including 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Allowed artifact/scratch root for this dispatch: ${plan.output_dir}'.
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract explicitly informs agents of the boundary before execution.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues validate that evidence records declare scope and provide project locators; missing these causes EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED or PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers.
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents agents from bypassing fact-checking requirements with unverified claims.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each evidence agent result, converting issues into blockers that prevent prd_ready=true.
evidence.why: Readiness validation explicitly checks all evidence results for scope/completeness issues before marking PRD-ready.

risks: If the boundary audit logic is buggy or has false negatives, agents could mutate project files without triggering blockers.
If allowed_write_roots is misconfigured to include target project directories, mutations would be allowed.

result.notes: Boundary audit is implemented across planning, execution, and readiness validation. Tests prove it detects git and non-git mutations while ignoring writes to allowed artifact roots. Scope validation prevents unverified project facts from becoming PRD-ready.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has a harness boundary audit that detects target-project file mutations by full-tool Claude agents and blocks PRD readiness on violations.

evidence.summary: fileDigest uses SHA-256 via crypto.createHash; buildBoundarySnapshot walks all git or filesystem files and hashes each one, skipping allowed roots.
evidence.why: Independent confirmation that the boundary mechanism uses cryptographic hashing for file integrity detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps and returns deleted, modified, and added changes.
evidence.why: Confirms the diff algorithm can detect all mutation types.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime calls buildBoundarySnapshot before and after agent execution, then runs diff and sets boundary.project_mutation='violated' on changes.
evidence.why: Runtime integration proof that boundary audit wraps agent execution.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers map changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force readiness.prd_ready=false when violations exist.
evidence.why: Explicit blocker injection logic prevents PRD readiness on violations.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates writeFileSync mutation, asserts status='blocked', project_mutation='violated', and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test proves boundary violations block even when agents return passing results.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' creates existing.txt, modifies it during agent run, writes to allowed runtimeDir, and asserts only the modification is detected.
evidence.why: Proof that non-git fallback works and correctly ignores allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan declares execution_policy.boundary.project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots=[repoRelative(outputDir)].
evidence.why: Plan-level boundary contract declaration precedes execution.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt hardcodes 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Allowed artifact/scratch root: ${output_dir}' in agent instructions.
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract explicitly communicates boundary before execution.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues check evidence records for scope declaration and project locators; missing these produces EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED or PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers.
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents unverified claims from passing readiness.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each evidence agent result, converting issues into blockers.
evidence.why: Readiness validation checks all evidence results before marking PRD-ready.

risks: If buildBoundarySnapshot file walking has false negatives (e.g., ignores files it should track), mutations could go undetected.
If allowed_write_roots includes project directories due to misconfiguration, mutations would be allowed.

result.notes: All explorer evidence confirmed via independent file reads. Boundary audit is implemented with SHA-256 hashing, before/after snapshots, git and non-git fallbacks, and explicit blocker injection. Tests prove violations block PRD readiness even with passing agent results. Scope validation enforces evidence quality standards.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has a harness boundary audit that detects target-project file mutations by full-tool Claude agents and blocks PRD readiness on violations.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both independently verified SHA-256 file digest, boundary snapshot building, diff comparison, runtime integration, blocker injection, planning declaration, and agent prompt contracts.
evidence.why: Cross-verified implementation across planning, execution, and readiness validation layers.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both verified tests covering git-backed boundary detection, non-git fallback, and project mutation blocking even with passing agent results.
evidence.why: Test coverage confirms boundary audit works across git and non-git contexts and actually blocks PRD readiness on violations.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both verified evidence scope validation and project locator requirements produce blockers preventing unverified claims from achieving PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Scope validation ensures evidence quality standards are enforced before PRD-ready status.

risks: If buildBoundarySnapshot file walking has false negatives, mutations could go undetected.
If allowed_write_roots misconfiguration includes project directories, mutations would be allowed.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is in complete agreement. All evidence records properly declare scope as 'project', include precise line numbers, provide clear summaries, and explain why each evidence matters. No assumptions are presented as facts. No missing data remains. Risks are appropriately acknowledged. The evidence sufficiently supports PRD readiness for the acceptance criterion that Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
